codeReview.summary: The r-integration feature correctly wires Rust P1 and P2 kernels into crossbasis() and crosspred() with automatic R fallback. The implementation follows the feature description precisely: (1) crossbasis.R dispatches to fused_crossbasis() via tryCatch with R loop fallback for time-series input, (2) crosspred.R dispatches lag-specific SE to quad_form_se() and cumulative/overall SE to cumulative_quad_form_se() with proper R fallback patterns. Tests comprehensively cover: Rust dispatch correctness, R fallback, partial availability (P1 Rust + P2 R fallback), full pipeline equivalence with 4-output comparison, and function signature/return type preservation. The 6.2x crossbasis speedup at 10MB C2 demonstrates the optimization works. All 111 tests pass.

issues.description: In the Rust cumulative SE path, cumfit is computed separately using a for loop but the Rust kernel could potentially compute this too. However, cumfit is just matrix multiplication (Xpredall %*% coef) which is already fast in R, so this is a minor optimization opportunity, not a bug.

The rust_cum_result assignment uses tryCatch returning NULL on error, then checks if(!is.null(...)). This pattern is correct but could be slightly cleaner using the same pattern as the matse_vec assignment (directly using fallback in error handler). However, the current pattern makes the code structure clearer for maintainability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges that rextendr::register_extendr() was not needed for R-only changes. This is accurate since this feature only modified R files, not Rust code. The skill procedure says to use register_extendr() after Rust changes, which is correct - but it could be clearer that it's only needed when Rust code is modified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The skill mentions using rextendr::register_extendr() after Rust changes, but for this feature (R-only changes) no Rust code was modified so this step was not needed'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file uses a with_disabled_rust() helper that unlocks/locks namespace bindings to temporarily replace Rust functions with throwing stubs. This is a useful pattern for testing fallback behavior that could be documented in AGENTS.md as a testing convention for R/Rust integration features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/testthat/test-r-integration.R:15-26 defines with_disabled_rust() helper using unlockBinding/lockBinding pattern

summary: PASS: The r-integration feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage. The worker correctly wired Rust P1 (fused_crossbasis) and P2 (quad_form_se, cumulative_quad_form_se) kernels into crossbasis() and crosspred() with tryCatch fallback to original R code. The implementation handles all edge cases from the feature description: grouped/ungrouped data, cumulative/non-cumulative modes, partial Rust availability, and preserves all function signatures and return types. All 111 tests pass, full pipeline equivalence verified with 11 all.equal() comparisons returning TRUE, and 6.2x speedup observed at 10MB C2. The code quality is good with clear comments marking the Rust dispatch sections. No blocking issues found; 2 minor observations noted.
